Tests the behavior of Object.freeze in strict mode.

# Purpose
This code defines a single function [`f_0`](<#pebble-firmware/src/fw/vendor/jerryscript/tests/jerry/regression-test-issue-359f_0>) that takes one parameter `a_0`. The function uses JavaScript's strict mode, which enforces stricter parsing and error handling on the code. Inside the function, the `Object.freeze` method is called on an object with a single property `a` set to the string `"foo"`. The `Object.freeze` method is used to make the object immutable, preventing any modifications to its properties. This code provides narrow functionality, primarily demonstrating the use of `Object.freeze` within a function.

### f\_0<!-- {{#callable:pebble-firmware/src/fw/vendor/jerryscript/tests/jerry/regression-test-issue-359.f_0}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../../src/fw/vendor/jerryscript/tests/jerry/regression-test-issue-359.js#L16>)

Freezes a newly created object with a single property.
- **Inputs**:
    - `a_0`: An input parameter that is not used in the function.
- **Control Flow**:
    - Enables strict mode for the function.
    - Creates a new object with a single property `a` set to the string 'foo'.
    - Freezes the newly created object to prevent modifications.
- **Output**: The function does not return any value.
